MOVING PARTY FAILED TO PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, IN VIOLATION OF LOCAL RULE 2.6B. ORDER RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANTS NICK LANHAM, ED KOSTER AND JENNIFER KOMM TO REPOSND TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD NO ORAL ARGUMENTS ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JOHN WONG'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANTS NICK LANHAM, ED KOSTER, AND JENNIFER KOMM TO RESPOND TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, AND IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. ABSENT A STAY (AS IN TERMINALS EQUIP., 221 CA3D 234, CITED BY DEFENDANTS), PENDENCY OF A DEMURRER IS NOT GROUNDS FOR FAILING TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES IS THEREFORE GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANTS LANHAM, KOSTER AND KOMM. PER CCP 2030.290, ALL OBJECTIONS ARE WAIVED. NO SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 15327075) FILED BY DEFENDANT LANHAM, NICK KOSTER, ED KOMM, JENNIFER

DEPT. 501: COURT ADOPTS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT NICK LANHAM, ED KOSTER, JENNIFER KOMM'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT THE CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST THE MOVING DEFENDANTS. THE COURT NOTES THAT OPPOSITION WAS FILED 3 COURTS DAYS LATE, I.E. 1 COURT DAY BEFORE THE REPLY WAS DUE). THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER TOT EH SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS ALSO LATE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY; CLERK J. RIOS MERIDA; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Judge: Ronald Evans Quidachay; Clerk J. Rios Merida; matter not reported. (D501)

Attorney, Jonathan Bornstein, Bornstein & Bornstein, 415-409-7611, plaintiff/opposing party
Attorneys, April Sun and Noah Jennings, Goddwin Procter LLP, 415-733-6014 for defendantdemurring party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court rules now as follows:

The Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT NICK LANHAM, ED KOSTER, JENNIFER KOMM DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Plaintiff fails to allege facts to support the causes of action against the moving defendants. The Court notes that opposition was filed 3 courts days late, i.e. 1 court day before the reply was due). The Court also notes that opposition to the demurrer tot eh Second Amended Complaint was also late. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-07-2015 Continued To Mar-02-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff John Wong'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendants Nick Lanham, Ed Koster, And Jennifer Komm To Respond To Form Interrogatories, And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jonathan Bornstein, Esq., (415)409-7611 of Bornstein & Bornstein fo Plaintiff/Moving party John Wong.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard no oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff John Wong'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendants Nick Lanham, Ed Koster, And Jennifer Komm To Respond To Form Interrogatories, And Imposing Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Absent a stay (as in Terminals Equip., 221 CA3d 234, cited by defendants), pendency of a demurrer is not grounds for failing to respond to discovery. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories is therefore GRANTED as to defendants Lanham, Koster and Komm. Per CCP 2030.290, all objections are waived. No sanctions. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
